title: How to trade safe with AI?
author: Nervous_Put5617
selftext:
Yo everyone,

AI trading is everywhere in 2026, but most “set it and forget it” bots still wreck accounts during dumps or weird news. I’ve messed around enough to see what’s legit for normal retail folks (stocks/crypto/options).

Quick reality check: AI is a killer tool for ideas, scans, and backtests — but it won’t magically print money. You still need risk rules and not go full degen.

Stuff that’s actually useful rn (March 2026):

1.  Free starters

	•  ChatGPT/Claude/Grok: Prompt them to write Pine Script, summarize news filings, or spit out simple strategies. Super handy for brainstorming.

	•  TradingView: Use AI to tweak indicators or find patterns faster.

2.  Paid tools worth it (from what people are actually using)

	•  Trade Ideas – Solid AI scanner/signals (Holly still crushes for day trades).

	•  TrendSpider – Auto trendlines + pattern detection, great charts.

	•  StockHero or Tickeron – Easy bot builders if you want semi-auto without coding.

	•  Danelfin/Kavout – For stock picks/ratings if you’re more swing/long-term.

Crypto side: 3Commas/Pionex grids or Bybit bots still popular, but watch leverage.

3.  Strategies where AI helps most

	•  Sentiment/news momentum plays (AI scans X/Reddit fast).

	•  Mean reversion or breakout signals.

	•  Auto alerts + strict stops.

Big warnings (seriously):

•  Overfitting kills backtests.

•  Most retail traders lose anyway — AI or not.

•  Start paper trading/small size for months.

•  1-2% risk max per trade.

•  No “guaranteed win” Telegram bots.

My simple starter plan:

Pick one thing (like BTC or a few stocks).

Build/test ONE basic idea with free AI.

Track trades in a Google Sheet.

Only scale if it’s positive after 50-100 trades.

AI gives an edge if you stay disciplined, but it’s not replacing good old risk management.

What AI tool or setup are you running that’s actually making you money (or not)? Horror stories welcome too lol.

Trade safe out there.

\#AITrading #Daytrading #AlgoTrading #Crypto

comments.author: Rabitai_Trades
comments.body: I write my own algos. Old school .py dev here. I use AI for prescreening and context only.

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body: yeah the “set and forget” bots are what blow ppl up imo. i’ve had way better luck just using AI to scan for setups and then manually sizing small + hard stops. once CPI or some random headline drops the bot logic just goes out the window lol.

comments.author: CitibagTradingDesk
comments.body:
Where most retail AI traders are trying to automate entries. We went the other way.

At Citibag Trading Desk we built AI around regime, flow, aligned pair selection, and permission-based execution. No context, no trade. No clean structure, no trade. No stable regime, reduced risk or stand aside. That is a lot safer than handing your account to a bot that thinks every candle is an opportunity. So no, we are not running “AI bots.” We built a decision architecture. The real edge is not automation. It is controlled permission 🫡

comments.author: No-Consequence-1779
comments.body: The calling ai bots but it’s really just range trading with limits. Ai can predict as well as a person. Mathematics is the best bet.  Must of ai is a solution looking for a problem.

comments.author: Quantum_Core_Trading
comments.body: Just make sure you set rules and kill switches if you are worried about blowing accounts. I haven’t blown accounts with bots in a very very long time and I use them on prop firms and no issue.
